channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitics headlines curated from Tier 1 sources

item.title: Taiwan denies receiving US military sales changes notification
item.description: Taiwan presidential office says no information about US adjusting military sales after acting US Navy Secretary suggests pause due to Iran war needs.

item.title: Belarus pledges not to join Ukraine war but will defend with Russia
item.description: President Lukashenko says Belarus will not be dragged into Ukraine conflict but will jointly defend against aggression with Russia after nuclear drills end.

item.title: Four killed in Ukrainian drone attack on Luhansk student dormitory
item.description: Russian officials report four dead and 35 children wounded in overnight Ukrainian drone strike on student dormitory in Russian-controlled Luhansk region.

item.title: UK police say Prince Andrew investigation will be lengthy
item.description: British police say investigation into King Charles brother Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor will be long and complex following arrest on misconduct allegations.

item.title: Irish PM to discuss Middle East conflict with Pope
item.description: Taoiseach Micheal Martin will discuss ongoing Middle East conflict and reconciliation process in Ireland during meeting with Pope.

item.title: US approves $108 million HAWK missile system sale to Ukraine
item.description: US State Department approves sale of HAWK missile system equipment and services to Ukraine worth $108 million to strengthen air defense capabilities.

item.title: Ukraine foreign minister meets new Hungarian counterpart
item.description: Ukraines foreign minister Andrii Sybiha meets Hungarian counterpart Peter Szijjarto to discuss minority rights and bilateral relations for the first time.

item.title: Hajj pilgrimage overshadowed by Middle East war tensions
item.description: Over one million pilgrims begin Hajj in Saudi Arabia as regional conflicts and geopolitical tensions cast shadow over the annual religious gathering.

item.title: Ukraine Strikes Russian Oil Refinery 700 km from Border
item.description: Ukrainian forces attacked a Russian oil refinery in Yaroslavl 700 km from the border President Zelenskiy said continuing strikes on Russian energy infrastructure.

item.title: Turkey Political Crisis Deepens as Opposition Vows Resistance
item.description: Turkey political crisis intensifies as opposition vows to resist court ruling annulling main opposition party CHP congress deepening domestic turmoil.

item.title: Oil Prices Rise as Investors Doubt US-Iran Breakthrough
item.description: US oil prices rise as investors remain skeptical about breakthrough in US-Iran peace talks despite ongoing diplomatic efforts.

item.title: China Cuts Oil Imports as Higher US Exports Wrongfoot Market Bulls
item.description: China cuts oil imports while higher US exports wrongfoot market bulls creating uncertainty in global oil markets.

item.title: Euro Zone Growth Set to Slow in 2026 as Middle East Conflict Fuels Inflation
item.description: Euro zone growth projected to slow in 2026 as Middle East conflict continues to fuel inflation pressures across the region.

item.title: Kremlin Denies China Trained Russian Soldiers for Ukraine War
item.description: Kremlin dismisses as false a Reuters report that China army secretly trained about 200 Russian soldiers in China late last year some of whom went on to fight in Ukraine.

item.title: Russian Refinery Partially Shuts After Ukrainian Drone Attack
item.description: Russias Norsi refinery partially shut down after Ukrainian drone attack sources say continuing pattern of strikes on Russian energy infrastructure.

item.title: Trump Vows US Will Retrieve Irans Enriched Uranium
item.description: President Donald Trump vowed that the United States will eventually recover Irans stockpile of highly enriched uranium despite Iranian refusal to hand over the material.

item.title: Exxon in Talks to Return to Venezuela After Two-Decade Absence
item.description: Exxon may acquire rights to produce oil in Venezuela after two-decade absence as geopolitical dynamics shift in the region.

item.title: Ireland to Introduce Bill Curbing Trade With Israeli Settlements
item.description: Ireland plans to introduce bill to curb trade with Israeli settlements in the coming weeks minister says reflecting growing international pressure.

item.title: Taiwan Denies Knowledge of US Arms Pause Despite Navy Statement
item.description: Taiwan denies receiving notification about US military sales changes despite acting US Navy Secretary suggesting pause due to Iran war priorities.

item.title: Netanyahu Leaked Secret UAE Wartime Visit to Boost His Image
item.description: Analysis of why Netanyahu leaked Israels secret wartime visit to UAE and how it was used to boost his political image.

item.title: Injured Palestinian Girls Arrive in Boston for Medical Treatment
item.description: Injured Palestinian girls from Gaza arrive in Boston for specialized medical treatment amid ongoing humanitarian crisis.

item.title: UK Refuses to Revoke Hong Kong Trade Office Privileges
item.description: UK official tells critical MPs that Hong Kong trade office can continue legitimate activity refusing to revoke privileges despite concerns.

item.title: Pakistan mediates US-Iran peace talks as uranium dispute continues
item.description: Irans foreign minister met Pakistans interior minister to discuss proposals to end the war as diplomatic efforts intensify.

item.title: Quad foreign ministers to meet in Delhi amid China tensions
item.description: Quad foreign ministers from US Japan Australia and India to meet in Delhi as regional tensions with China continue.

item.title: Turkish court ousts main opposition leader Ozel
item.description: Turkish court annuls main opposition CHP partys 2023 congress dealing major blow to opposition leader Ozgur Ozel.
